error_signature: duplicate cron executions matching worker count
title: Duplicate Cron executions due to worker misconfiguration
symptom: Cron triggers execute multiple times, matching the number of worker instances. This leads to duplicated executions and unreliable automation.
applies_when: Running n8n in queue mode with multiple workers
not_this_if: Workers are correctly configured to run as 'n8n worker'
root_cause: When worker containers are started with the default command 'n8n start', they behave as additional main instances. This results in each worker independently registering and activating Cron triggers, leading to multiple executions.
fix_steps: Access the worker service configuration in your deployment setup.
Set the command for the worker services to 'n8n worker --concurrency=5'.
agent_instruction: Change the worker command to 'n8n worker --concurrency=5' and redeploy the workers.
